in_scope: Data centre consolidation of Sites A, B, C

drift_test_within_scope.txt.rationale: Email asks about network switch delivery timeline — directly in scope (network equipment migration)

drift_test_scope_expansion.txt.rationale: Request to add Wi-Fi access point upgrades — explicitly excluded as 'network monitoring tool upgrades' and not in scope. Represents scope expansion.

drift_test_new_requirement.txt.rationale: Request to add a new disaster recovery site — not mentioned anywhere in the SOW. Entirely new requirement with significant cost and schedule implications.
drift_test_new_requirement.txt.key_conflict: No DR site in SOW scope or deliverables

drift_test_contradicts.txt.rationale: ADM decision extends deadline to Q4 2026 (SOW says April 30, 2026) and increases budget to $3.5M (SOW says $2.4M). Directly contradicts both budget and timeline constraints.
drift_test_contradicts.txt.key_conflict: Violates budget constraint ($2.4M) and timeline constraint (Q2 2026)

drift_test_ambiguous.txt.rationale: SolarWinds NPM upgrade could be interpreted as in-scope (required to monitor in-scope network equipment) or out-of-scope (network monitoring tool upgrade explicitly excluded). Reasonable arguments exist for both interpretations.
drift_test_ambiguous.txt.key_conflict: SOW excludes 'network monitoring tool upgrades' but the upgrade is needed for in-scope network equipment

drift_test_clean.txt.rationale: Status update on AD migration preparation — entirely within scope activities (AD migration, DC staging, GPO consolidation, testing)

drift_test_fr_scope_creep.txt.rationale: French-language request to add end-user device refresh (85 laptops, 40 desktops) — explicitly out of scope in SOW section 2.2(a). Tests bilingual drift detection.
drift_test_fr_scope_creep.txt.key_conflict: SOW explicitly excludes 'end-user device refresh or replacement'
